req.title: Data retention
req.statement: Resources shall be retained per policy; expired resources shall be purged on a nightly job with full audit trail.

doc.reasoning: Data retention and purging is a critical healthcare compliance function. The requirement mandates that expired resources be purged nightly with full audit trail and incident escalation on policy mismatch. Test coverage must verify: (1) successful purge of expired resources with audit logging (positive), (2) failure modes when policy rules are violated or audit fails (negative), (3) boundary conditions around retention window edges and policy thresholds (boundary). All tests target the /cron/retention endpoint and validate both data state changes and audit trail creation, per HIPAA compliance obligations.

tests.name: test_retention_positive_expired_resources_purged_with_audit
preconditions: Database contains 3 resources: 2 expired (creation_date > retention_days ago), 1 current
actions: Query audit log for purge events
expected: Audit log contains 2 entries with action="PURGE" and resource_ids matching deleted resources
Each audit entry includes timestamp, actor="system_job", and policy_version
trace: R-HLT-020: Resources shall be retained per policy
R-HLT-020: expired resources shall be purged on a nightly job
tests.executable:
import requests
import json
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def test_retention_positive_expired_resources_purged_with_audit(api_base_url, db_connection):
    # Setup: insert expired and current resources
    expired_date = (datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(days=35)).isoformat()
    current_date = (datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(days=5)).isoformat()
    <setup_resources_in_db>(
        db_connection, 
        [("res-001", expired_date), ("res-002", expired_date), ("res-003", current_date)]
    )
    
    response = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/cron/retention",
        json={"policy_id": "default"},
        headers={"Authorization": "Bearer <system_token>"}
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert response.json()["status"] == "purge_job_completed"
    
    remaining = <query_resource_count>(db_connection)
    assert remaining == 1
    
    audit_entries = <query_audit_log>(db_connection, action="PURGE")
    assert len(audit_entries) == 2
    assert all(e["actor"] == "system_job" for e in audit_entries)

tests.name: test_retention_negative_policy_mismatch_raises_incident
preconditions: Database contains 5 resources with mixed expiry dates
Audit log write permission is REVOKED for system_job user
actions: Monitor incident queue for raised incidents
expected: Response includes error message mentioning policy mismatch or audit failure
Incident raised in incident queue with severity="HIGH" and category="compliance"
Database transaction rolled back; all 5 resources remain intact
trace: R-HLT-020: incident raised on policy mismatch
tests.executable:
import requests
import json

def test_retention_negative_policy_mismatch_raises_incident(api_base_url, db_connection, incident_client):
    initial_count = <query_resource_count>(db_connection)
    
    response = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/cron/retention",
        json={"policy_id": "default"},
        headers={"Authorization": "Bearer <system_token>"}
    )
    assert response.status_code == 500
    assert "AUDIT_WRITE_FAILED" in response.json()["error_code"]
    
    final_count = <query_resource_count>(db_connection)
    assert final_count == initial_count
    
    incidents = incident_client.list_incidents()
    assert len(incidents) >= 1
    assert any(
        i["severity"] == "HIGH" and i["category"] == "compliance" 
        for i in incidents
    )

tests.name: test_retention_boundary_resource_at_exact_retention_threshold
preconditions: Database contains resources with creation timestamps:
expected: Audit log shows exactly 1 purge entry for Resource B
Purge timestamp in audit is recorded with millisecond precision
trace: R-HLT-020: expired resources shall be purged
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4: Boundary Value Analysis for retention window edge cases
tests.executable:
import requests
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def test_retention_boundary_resource_at_exact_retention_threshold(api_base_url, db_connection):
    now = datetime.utcnow()
    retention_days = 30
    
    <setup_resources_in_db>(db_connection, [
        ("res-at-threshold", (now - timedelta(days=30)).isoformat()),
        ("res-past-threshold", (now - timedelta(days=30, seconds=1)).isoformat()),
        ("res-before-threshold", (now - timedelta(days=29, minutes=59)).isoformat())
    ])
    
    response = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/cron/retention",
        json={"policy_id": "default"},
        headers={"Authorization": "Bearer <system_token>"}
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    
    remaining = <query_resources_by_id>(db_connection, 
                  ["res-at-threshold", "res-past-threshold", "res-before-threshold"])
    assert len(remaining) == 2
    assert "res-at-threshold" in [r["id"] for r in remaining]
    assert "res-past-threshold" not in [r["id"] for r in remaining]
    assert "res-before-threshold" in [r["id"] for r in remaining]
    
    audit_entries = <query_audit_log>(db_connection, action="PURGE")
    assert len(audit_entries) == 1
    assert audit_entries[0]["resource_id"] == "res-past-threshold"

tests.name: test_retention_boundary_empty_database_no_purge_needed
expected: Single audit entry created with action="PURGE_JOB_RUN" and purged_count=0
trace: R-HLT-020: nightly job runs successfully with no work
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_retention_boundary_empty_database_no_purge_needed(api_base_url, db_connection):
    <clear_all_resources>(db_connection)
    <clear_audit_log>(db_connection)
    
    response = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/cron/retention",
        json={"policy_id": "default"},
        headers={"Authorization": "Bearer <system_token>"}
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    assert response.json()["status"] == "purge_job_completed"
    assert response.json()["resources_purged_count"] == 0
    
    audit_entries = <query_audit_log>(db_connection, action="PURGE_JOB_RUN")
    assert len(audit_entries) == 1
    assert audit_entries[0]["purged_count"] == 0

tests.name: test_retention_negative_malformed_policy_id_rejected
preconditions: Retention policy endpoint requires valid policy_id parameter
actions: Inspect response status and error details
trace: R-HLT-020: policy validation prior to purge job execution
ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4: Decision Table for invalid input combinations
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_retention_negative_malformed_policy_id_rejected(api_base_url, db_connection):
    initial_count = <query_resource_count>(db_connection)
    
    response = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/cron/retention",
        json={"policy_id": "invalid@policy#id"},
        headers={"Authorization": "Bearer <system_token>"}
    )
    assert response.status_code == 400
    assert "INVALID_POLICY_ID" in response.json()["error_code"]
    
    final_count = <query_resource_count>(db_connection)
    assert final_count == initial_count
    
    audit_entries = <query_audit_log>(db_connection)
    assert len(audit_entries) == 0
